name: Congruence and Similarity
whatIsIt: Two shapes are CONGRUENT if they are exactly the same shape AND size — one fits perfectly on the other (you may flip or turn it). They are SIMILAR if they are the same shape but different size — one is a scaled copy, with equal angles and sides in the same ratio. Congruent = identical twins; similar = a photo and its enlargement.
whyNeeded: Congruence and similarity explain scale drawings, maps, enlargements, shadows, and why matching parts work in design and engineering. Similar triangles are also the idea behind trigonometry and finding unknown heights and distances.
realLifeUses: A photo and its bigger print are similar
A map is similar to the real land (scaled down)
Finding a tall tree's height from its shadow using similar triangles

vocabulary.meaning: Same shape and same size — an exact match.
Same shape, different size — a scaled copy with equal angles.
Matching sides or angles in the two shapes.

story.title: The Photo and the Poster
story.text: Kabir has a small photo and a big poster of the same picture. 'Are they the same?' asks Didi. 'Same shape, different size — they're SIMILAR,' says Kabir. Then he finds two identical stamps. 'These fit exactly on each other — congruent!' Didi nods: 'Similar is scaled; congruent is a perfect match.'
story.extractedProblem: A photo and its enlarged poster show the same picture at different sizes. Are they congruent or similar?

visual.caption: Both rectangles are the same shape; the second is a ×2 scaled copy → similar. Same size too would be congruent.

teachingGallery.title: Match or scale
teachingGallery.note: Congruent = same size; similar = same shape, scaled.

examples.caption: Sides in the same ratio → similar.

standardMethod.summary: Compare angles and sides. Equal angles and equal sides → congruent. Equal angles and sides in the same ratio → similar.
steps: Line up corresponding parts (matching sides and angles).
If ALL corresponding sides AND angles are equal → CONGRUENT.
If angles are equal and sides are in the SAME ratio (but a different size) → SIMILAR.
In similar shapes, use the scale factor to find a missing side (multiply or divide).

workedExamples.problem: Two triangles have all equal angles and one is twice the size of the other. Congruent or similar?
steps: So they are SIMILAR, not congruent.

workedExamples.problem: A small triangle has base 3 cm; a similar one is scaled ×4. What is its base?
steps: Similar → multiply by the scale factor.

commonMistakes.fix: Congruent means SAME SIZE too. Different sizes with the same shape are only SIMILAR.

commonMistakes.mistake: Scaling only one side of a similar shape.
commonMistakes.fix: In similar shapes, EVERY side scales by the same factor — keep the ratio the same on all sides.

easy.q: Two shapes that are exactly the same shape AND size are…
A photo and its bigger print are…

medium.q: A similar shape is scaled ×3. A 5 cm side becomes how many cm?
Two identical stamps placed on each other are…

challenge.q: A model is 1/50 of real size. A real car is 300 cm long. The model's length (cm)?
Ravi says a shape and its ×2 enlargement are congruent. What's the fix?

options.label: They're similar — congruent needs the same size

challenge.answer: They're similar — congruent needs the same size

questions.q: Same shape but different size means…
Same shape AND same size means…
A similar shape scaled ×5. A 4 cm side becomes (cm)?
In similar shapes, the corresponding angles are…

teachBackPrompt: Find two objects at home that are congruent (like two identical coins) and two that are similar (a small and a large plate, or a photo and its print). Explain to a grown-up how congruent means exact match and similar means scaled copy.

revisionCard.summary: Congruent = same shape AND size (exact match, may flip/turn). Similar = same shape, different size (scaled; equal angles, sides in the same ratio). In similar shapes, every side scales by the same factor — use it to find a missing side.

rememberIt.hook: Congruent = same shape AND size. Similar = same shape, scaled
rememberIt.unpack: Congruent copies match exactly (SSS, SAS, ASA, RHS). Similar shapes share equal angles and sides in the same ratio.

studentQuestions.a: Congruent = identical (same size). Similar = same shape but bigger or smaller (scaled).
A shortcut proving two triangles identical: SSS, SAS, ASA, or RHS - without checking all six measurements.
